title: AMC Q1 Preview: Meme Stock Rally Meets Reality Check
text:
AMC Entertainment is anticipated to report first-quarter revenue of approximately $974.61 million, representing growth from $862.50 million in the previous year's Q1. The company has demonstrated a strong track record, having beaten analyst estimates for revenue in three straight quarters and in eight of the past 10 quarters overall.

ANALYST SENTIMENT
Analysts project an earnings loss of 33 cents per share, an improvement compared to the 58-cent loss from last year's first quarter.

Recent analyst ratings from February 2026 remain cautious:
- Macquarie: Neutral rating with $1.50 price target
- Citigroup: Sell rating with $1.10 price target
- Roth Capital: Neutral rating with $1.50 price target

KEY PERFORMANCE DRIVERS
AMC enters earnings with several positive catalysts. The first-quarter domestic box office surged 24.8% year-over-year to $1.78 billion, bolstered by blockbuster releases like Project Hail Mary. The company achieved record Easter period attendance and merchandise sales across its global locations, including over six million visitors during that five-day span.

STOCK MOMENTUM
AMC shares experienced significant April gains, rising 55% that month—the largest monthly surge since June 2021. As of the latest close, the stock traded at $1.45, down 9% year-to-date.

2026 MOVIE PIPELINE
Management is likely highlighting the robust 2026 release schedule featuring major franchises including Star Wars, Toy Story, Spider-Man, Avengers, and Dune—predominantly releasing during summer months.
